new_classes.label_en: Other wine (not PDO, not PGI, not variety/vintage-labelled, other than white) in containers ≤ 2 l — other (CN 2204 21 98)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Wein (ohne g.U., ohne g.g.A., ohne Rebsorte/Jahrgang, außer Weißwein) in Behältnissen mit einem Inhalt von ≤ 2 l — anderer (KN 2204 21 98)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes (other than sparkling and semi-sparkling), not bearing a PDO or PGI designation, not labelled with a vine variety or vintage year, other than white wine, presented in containers holding 2 litres or less. Classified under CN 2204 21 98. The non-volatile constituents of the total dry extract include sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts (see additional notes 4 and 5 of Chapter 22).

new_classes.label_en: Wine without PDO, PGI, vine variety or vintage year designation quality
new_classes.label_de: Wein ohne g.U., g.g.A., Rebsorten- oder Jahrgangsangabe – Qualitätsmerkmal
new_classes.definition_en: Quality borne by a wine that carries neither a Protected Designation of Origin (PDO/g.U.) nor a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI/g.g.A.) nor a vine variety nor a vintage year indication on its label or documentation.

new_classes.label_en: Non-white wine colour quality (red or rosé)
new_classes.definition_en: Colour quality borne by a wine that is not white wine; i.e. it is red or rosé wine. Used to discriminate CN 2204 21 98 (other, non-white) from white wine subheadings within the same container-size bracket.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 21 98 are captured: (1) wine of fresh grapes, non-sparkling, non-semi-sparkling, (2) in containers ≤ 2 l (SmallContainer restriction), (3) not PDO, not PGI, not vine-variety/vintage labelled, (4) other than white wine (NonWhiteWineColourQuality). These map to the legal text scope "22042111 to 22042198 — Other" under 22042100, with the additional notes 4 and 5 references noted in TotalDryExtractQuality already present in the TBox. The coverage is slightly reduced (0.82) because: (a) the full enumeration of sibling subheadings excluded by the 'other' catch-all (e.g. 22042111–22042177 white wines, 22042174/68 PDO red/rosé, 22042180 PGI, 22042178 other no-PDO/PGI white) cannot all be expressed as complement restrictions without those sibling classes being fully defined in this session; (b) the precise quantitative thresholds from additional notes 4 and 5 (total dry extract limits distinguishing between sub-subheadings) are referenced in the legal text but the specific numeric values for this catch-all 'other' node are not unambiguously stated in the provided legal text excerpt, so full formalisation of those thresholds is not possible from this source alone.
